welcome.title: Welcome to Lurus Switch
welcome.subtitle: Pick your operating mode (changeable later in Settings)
welcome.hint: Not sure? Most individual developers should choose Personal.

personal.label: Personal
personal.desc: Solo developer: install Claude Code / Codex / Gemini, manage proxies and configs, talk to the Lurus-operated LLM gateway.

reseller.label: Reseller
reseller.tagline: I run an AI tooling shop
reseller.desc: Distributor: deploy your own Hub in one click, configure model routing, batch-issue activation codes, export white-labeled clients.

enterprise.label: Enterprise
enterprise.desc: Internal IT: SSO sign-in, per-cost-center token accounting, DLP middleware on the gateway, monthly chargeback reports that drop into SAP / Workday.

enduser.label: EndUser
enduser.desc: Customer: activate the client your reseller gave you, install CLI tools automatically, get to work.

activate.title: Activate your service
activate.subtitle: Enter the redemption code from your reseller
activate.deviceNote: The redemption code is bound to this device. Switching devices requires re-activation.
activate.help: No code? Contact the reseller who gave you this installer.

error.invalidInput: Invalid input — please check the code format.
error.network: Unable to reach the Hub. Check your network and retry.
error.notFound: Redemption code is unknown or invalid.
error.used: This code has already been redeemed. Contact your reseller.
error.expired: This code has expired. Contact your reseller.
error.disabled: This code was disabled by the reseller.
error.endpointAbsent: This Hub does not yet expose the redemption endpoint. Contact the reseller.
error.server: Service is temporarily unavailable. Please retry shortly.

action.noSupport: Please contact the reseller who shipped you this installer.

main.title: My service
main.subtitle: Quota, connection status, and CLI tool launchers.
main.noTools: No CLI tools installed yet — head to the Tools page.
main.resetConfirm: Reset activation? Local credentials will be wiped and the next launch will ask for a new code.
main.staleHint: We've been unable to reach the Hub for a while. Service is still usable, but please check your network.
main.expiringSoonHint: Reach out to your reseller now to renew and avoid an outage.
main.expiringCriticalHint: Contact your reseller immediately to renew, or service will stop.

dlp.title: DLP — Data Loss Prevention
dlp.subtitle: Scans every request flowing through the local gateway and blocks / redacts / logs sensitive data. Changes apply immediately.

patterns.title: Rules
patterns.hint: Block rejects the request; Redact replaces matched bytes with a token; Warn / Allow only logs.

tester.title: Ad-hoc scan
tester.placeholder: Paste a prompt here to test what rules would fire.

recent.title: Recent hits
recent.hint: Ring buffer (last 200), populated by the gateway request path.

orgchart.title: Organization — departments & employees
orgchart.subtitle: Enterprise-mode chargeback, SSO binding, per-department token authorization.

employees.title: Employees
employees.noDept: Pick a department on the left

import.title: Bulk employee import (SCIM-lite)
import.hint1: Paste CSV with columns: email, display_name, department, role. Header row optional (a row containing "email" is detected automatically).
import.hint2: Department column accepts a name (case-insensitive) or an ID. Empty falls back to the department selected on the left.
import.hint3: Existing emails are updated (DisplayName / Department / Role overwritten; SSO subject untouched); missing rows are created.

chargeback.title: Chargeback report
chargeback.subtitle: Token usage rolled up by department and employee. To populate the buckets, first bind each Connected App to an employee.
chargeback.empty: No records in this range. Bind apps to employees / departments and let traffic flow through the local gateway.

agentGallery.title: Agent template gallery
agentGallery.subtitle: 5 built-in role recipes — sales / support / ops / finance / compliance. Click to inspect system prompt, capability allowlist, budget, and guardrails.

common.unsavedChangesConfirm: You have unsaved changes on this page. Leave anyway?

competingInstall.title: Other tool configurations detected
competingInstall.body: Found existing {{names}} on this machine. Switch works alongside them — see the Tools page for a unified view.

header.themeToggleHint: Theme (click to cycle): dark → light → auto

setup.doneDesc: You can now configure channels, issue redemption codes, and view logs in Gateway Admin.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: App preferences
settings.modeSwitchUserBody: Gateway management and promoter features will be hidden. You can switch back anytime in Settings.
settings.modeSwitchPromoterBody: Gateway management and promoter features will appear, for running a local gateway and managing referrals.
settings.userLevelDesc: Controls how much detail is shown in the interface

appearance.startupPageDesc: Default page shown when app launches

proxy.movedNotice: Relay API config (key / endpoint) has been moved to Account > Connection.
proxy.hint: Tip: Go to Account page to configure relay API settings.

upstreamProxy.title: Upstream Proxy (BYO VPN)
upstreamProxy.subtitle: Every outbound HTTP request (AI relay, auto-updater, version checks) will be routed through this proxy. Switch ships no censorship-evasion logic; it only forwards through your own V2Ray / Clash / corporate proxy.
upstreamProxy.urlHint: Supports http / https / socks5 / socks5h (socks5h is recommended so DNS is resolved by the proxy)
upstreamProxy.noProxyHint: Comma-separated host suffixes. localhost / 127.0.0.0/8 / ::1 are always bypassed.
upstreamProxy.testUrlHint: URL probed when you click Test. Empty = default (Google 204).
upstreamProxy.detectNone: No local proxy detected. Start V2Ray / Clash / plugins/dnstunnel client first.
upstreamProxy.help1: If your network can't reach Anthropic / GitHub / npm directly, run V2Ray, Clash, Shadowsocks, etc. locally and put its listen address here.
upstreamProxy.help2: Switch does exactly one thing: it hands every HTTP request to the proxy you configured. It never bundles VPN, DNS-tunnel, or any circumvention transport.
upstreamProxy.help3: Changes apply live — no restart needed. New requests use the new proxy; in-flight connections keep their existing transport until done.

update.autoCheckDesc: Automatically check for new Lurus Switch versions at startup
update.toolUpdateHint: Tool updates (Claude/Codex/Gemini/PicoClaw/NullClaw) can be triggered from Home or Tools page.

data.title: Data Management
data.warning: The following operations cannot be undone. Please proceed with caution.
data.clearSnapshotsDesc: Delete all tool config snapshot history

doctor.title: Connectivity Doctor
doctor.subtitle: Probes AI providers, Lurus Hub, and infra services in parallel. Compares direct vs through-upstream-proxy reachability and surfaces one-click remedies.

desc.operations: Topup links, quotas, branding, registration policy — global controls that affect every user.
desc.payment: Payment provider keys and webhooks (Epay / Stripe / Creem / Waffo).
desc.pricing: Model ratios, group discounts, per-call pricing — visual editor or raw JSON.
desc.rateLimit: Global API / Web rate limits and per-model / per-group throttling.
desc.modelConfig: Model behavior switches: original request, safety, sensitive words.
desc.modelDeploy: Channel auto-disable/enable, usable groups, status code rules.
desc.performance: Retries, cache, batch updates, statistics aggregation.
desc.system: SMTP, third-party OAuth, captcha, file/image permissions.

editor.noEntries: No entries — click "Add model" to start

guide.cursor: Open Cursor → Settings → Models → Override OpenAI Base URL → paste the endpoint URL, then set API key to the app token
guide.windsurf: Open Windsurf → Settings → AI Provider → Custom → paste the endpoint URL and app token
guide.continue: Edit ~/.continue/config.json → set "apiBase" to the endpoint URL and "apiKey" to the app token
guide.cline: Open VS Code → Cline settings → set API Base URL and API Key

breakdown.empty: No usage recorded in the last 30 days

usage.monthCostEstimated: Estimated — extrapolated from today's usage; swaps to real value when Hub field lands

proxyPanel.fetchModelsHint: Query /v1/models to discover available model IDs

hooks.emptyHint: No hooks yet, click below to add

gatewayGuard.title: Gateway Not Running
gatewayGuard.desc: This feature requires the gateway to be running

banner.title: What is an Agent?
banner.body: Bind a CLI tool to a long-lived role: custom system prompt, dedicated model, isolated config dir, optional MCP servers and permissions. Compared to bare `claude` / `codex`, agents give you an out-of-the-box multi-agent fleet — stop and restart preserves identity; clone for A/B variants.

howItWorks.title: Get started in 3 steps
howItWorks.step1: Create: pick a tool + model + role name (e.g. "Frontend Reviewer")
howItWorks.step2: Launch: runs in an isolated config dir — no cross-contamination
howItWorks.step3: Reuse: stop and restart keeps the identity; clone for variants

detail.title: Agent detail
detail.noOutput: No output yet. Once the agent runs, stdout/stderr stream here.

gateway.title: What is the Gateway?
gateway.body: The gateway is a local API proxy that routes all your AI tool requests through a single endpoint. It handles authentication, model routing, usage tracking, and lets you switch between providers without reconfiguring each tool.

healthScore.title: Health Score
healthScore.body: A composite score (0-100) measuring how well your AI development environment is configured. It checks runtime dependencies, tool installations, configuration health, gateway connectivity, and system environment.

proxy.title: API Proxy Settings
proxy.body: The proxy routes your AI tool requests through a relay server. Set your API endpoint and token here. If you have a Lurus account, your token is provided automatically.

tools.title: AI CLI Tools
tools.body: These are command-line AI coding assistants. Each tool has its own config file. Lurus Switch manages them all from one place — install, configure, connect to the gateway, and keep them updated.

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.body: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers extend AI tools with additional capabilities like file access, web browsing, or database queries. Add presets here and they'll be injected into your tool configs.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.body: Manage prompt templates, context documents (like CLAUDE.md), and monitor running AI tool processes — all in one place.

autostart.title: Auto-start
autostart.body: When enabled, Lurus Switch launches minimized when your computer starts. The gateway will auto-start too if configured, so your AI tools are always ready.

envVar.title: Environment Variables
envVar.body: Some AI tools read API keys from environment variables. Lurus Switch can set these for you system-wide so tools work even outside this app.

pathConfig.title: PATH Configuration
pathConfig.body: Your system PATH tells the terminal where to find programs. If a tool is installed but 'command not found', its directory needs to be added to PATH.

toolConfig.notInstalledHint: Config changes won't take effect until the tool is installed

relay.title: Relay Management
relay.subtitle: Configure API relay endpoints for CLI tools — optional

intro.title: What is a relay?
intro.body: A relay is an alternative API endpoint to Anthropic / OpenAI official APIs. Switch forwards your CLI requests through it; the relay proxies to the upstream model.
intro.defaultBody: All tools go to Lurus' own API (hub.lurus.cn). If everything works, leave this page alone.
intro.whenUseBody: You bought a third-party relay or self-hosted newapi / OpenRouter and want certain tools to use it.

relay.recommendedHint: Vetted third-party relays — click "Use" to add one to your list.
relay.fetchHint: Click "Fetch from Cloud" to load recommended relay list
relay.customHint: Your own API service / internal relay. Needs URL + (optional) API key.
relay.deleteBlockedHint: Stop using it in Tool Routing first, then delete
relay.toolRoutingHint: Override the relay per CLI. Leave "Default" to use the global upstream — most users never touch this.

promptLib.title: Prompt Library
promptLib.selectHint: Select a prompt to see details
